questions.question: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Gallivant
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is: Meander Explanation: • Meander means to wander aimlessly or travel around with no fixed route, which is similar to gallivant, meaning to roam or travel for pleasure. • Brave means showing courage, which is not related to wandering. • Grave refers to something serious or a burial place, which does not match the meaning. • Gall refers to boldness or rudeness, different from gallivant.

questions.question: Select the word segment that substitutes (replaces) the bracketed word segment correctly and completes the sentence meaningfully. In case no improvement is needed, select ‘No improvement’. (Hardly had the news leak out that) my telephone started ringing.
options: No sooner had the news leaked out than
No sooner had the news leaked out that
No sooner did the news leaked out than
questions.explanation: The correct option to replace the bracketed word segment "Hardly had the news leak out that" and complete the sentence meaningfully is: No sooner had the news leaked out than No sooner had the news leaked out than: This option correctly uses the structure "No sooner...than" to indicate that one event happened immediately after another. It effectively conveys the sense of timing in the sentence.

questions.question: Choose the option that is the correct indirect form of the sentence. My mother said, “I am meeting your father today.”
options: The mother said that she has been meeting my father that day.
My mother says that she was meeting my father today.
My mother said that she was meeting my father that day.
My mother said that she were meeting my father that day.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c: “My mother said that she was meeting my father that day.” Direct speech ---- Indirect speech Present continuous ---- Past continuous Therefore, the correct indirect form of the sentence is: “My mother said that she was meeting my father that day.”

questions.question: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. The researchers P. performed experiments and developed a model Q. which explains how the worm's taste sensors R. to direct its foraging behaviour S. process information from the environment
questions.explanation: The correct order of the parts of the sentence is: PQSR This arrangement maintains the logical flow of the sentence. First, it introduces the action performed by the researchers ("performed experiments and developed a model"), followed by the explanation of what the model does ("which explains how the worm's taste sensors process information from the environment"). Finally, it concludes with the purpose or outcome of this process ("to direct its foraging behavior"). So, "PQSR" is indeed the correct answer.

questions.question: Parts of the following sentence are given as options. Identify the part that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select 'No error'. This was no strange sight / to the boy by that time, / for he had neither gun or spear.
options: to the boy by that time,
for he had neither gun or spear.
questions.explanation: The part that contains a grammatical error is: for he had neither gun or spear. The error lies in the incorrect use of the conjunction "or." When presenting two alternatives, the correct conjunction to use is "nor," not "or." Therefore, the corrected sentence would be: “This was no strange sight to the boy by that time, for he had neither gun nor spear.”

questions.question: Some parts of a few sentences have been jumbled up, and labelled A, B, C and D. Select the option that gives the correct sequence in which these parts can be rearranged to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence. There were two final A. the story was the tale of Kunti B. a story and a parchment. C. gifts the sorceress gave me D. mother of Arjun. The parchment was a map of Bharat’s many kingdoms
questions.explanation: The correct sequence to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence is: CBAD So, the rearranged sentence would be: "The gifts the sorceress gave me were two final: the story was the tale of Kunti, a mother of Arjun. The parchment was a map of Bharat’s many kingdoms."

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. He was a handsome man in his ______ thirties.
questions.explanation: The appropriate option to fill in the blank is: Late Late: Belonging to the latter part of a period of time; occurring near the end of a particular age range or time frame. Lest: For fear that; to prevent any possibility that something undesirable should happen. Least: Smallest in amount, extent, or significance; lowest in importance or degree. Latter: Referring to the second of two things or persons mentioned.

questions.question: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. Meghna has taken all the cash.
options: All the cash has been taken by Meghna.
All the cash was taken by Meghna.
All the cash had been taking by Meghna.
All the cash had been taken by Meghna.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option a: All the cash has been taken by Meghna. Active Voice ---- Passive voice Subject + has/have + V3 + object ---- object + has/have + been + V3 + by + subject Therefore, the corrected sentence would be: “All the cash has been taken by Meghna.”

questions.question: Improve the underlined part of the sentence. Choose ‘No improvement’ as an answer if the sentence is grammatically correct. This heap of rubbish has not b̲e̲e̲n̲ ̲r̲e̲m̲o̲v̲e̲ ̲f̲o̲r̲ 6 months.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: been removed for The underlined part of the sentence "been removed for" is grammatically incorrect. The correct form should be "been removed for" to indicate the duration of time the heap of rubbish has remained unremoved. So, the improved version of the sentence is: "This heap of rubbish has not been removed for 6 months."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Slack off
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: To ease pressure on something The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "slack off" is: To make most effort The idiom "slack off" means to decrease in activity or effort, to become less diligent or attentive to tasks. It often implies a decline in productivity or performance due to a lack of effort or motivation.

questions.question: Select the word that is similar in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below Glib
questions.explanation: The word that is similar in meaning (synonym) to "glib" is: Easy Glib: Fluent and voluble but insincere and shallow; superficially charming or persuasive. Inarticulate: Unable to express oneself clearly or fluently; lacking the ability to communicate effectively. Sincere: Genuine, honest, and truthful; without deceit or pretense. Implausible: Not seeming reasonable or probable; unlikely to be true or believable. Easy: Not difficult or demanding; requiring little effort or exertion.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the given four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. Pig in a poke
options: A brave man amongst all the cowards
Something that is bought without examining properly
To bring something to an end
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c: Something that is bought without examining properly The idiom "Pig in a poke" refers to something that is bought or accepted without first being properly examined or evaluated.

questions.question: Select the word that is closest in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below. ANXIOUS
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: Apprehensive Anxious: Experiencing worry, nervousness, or unease about something with an uncertain outcome. Apprehensive: Anxious or fearful that something bad or unpleasant will happen. Collected: Calm and composed, especially in stressful situations; not easily flustered or agitated. Flawless: Without any imperfections or errors; perfect in quality or execution. Accept: To agree to take or receive something that is offered; to approve or agree to a proposal or suggestion.

questions.question: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose ‘No error’. When we cook garlic, the sulphide molecules raise into the air and fill the room with their pleasing aroma.
options: When we cook garlic, the sulphide
and fill the room with their pleasing aroma.
questions.explanation: The part of the sentence that has an error is: molecules raise into the air "Raise" is a transitive verb, which means it requires an object to act upon. It means to lift or elevate something. "Rise," on the other hand, is an intransitive verb, which means it does not require an object. It means to move upward or ascend. So, the corrected sentence is: "When we cook garlic, the sulphide molecules rise into the air and fill the room with their pleasing aroma."

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: The correctly spelled word is: VACILLATE Vacillate: To waver between different opinions or actions; to be indecisive or irresolute. Maintenance: The process of preserving or sustaining something, or the state of being preserved or sustained. Obsequious: Excessively obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree; overly submissive or eager to please. Stilettoes: High-heeled shoes with a thin, pointed heel, typically worn by women.

questions.question: Choose the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelled word is: PERCIEVE (the correct spelling is "PERCEIVE") • Boisterous describes noisy and lively behaviour. • Viscous describes the thickness or stickiness of a liquid. • Benevolent describes a kind and well-meaning nature. • Perceive describes the act of becoming aware of something through the senses.

questions.question: Select the word that is opposite in meaning (ANTONYM) to the word given below. Fiend
questions.explanation: The word that is opposite in meaning (antonym) to "Fiend" is: Saint • Fiend describes someone who is very enthusiastic or obsessed with something. • Saint describes someone who is exceptionally good or virtuous. • Atrocious describes something that is extremely cruel, brutal, or shockingly bad. • Vile describes something that is extremely unpleasant, foul, or morally reprehensible. • Junkie describes someone who is addicted to or excessively fond of something, often used derogatorily.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select ‘No improvement’. Africa has glaciers on t̲h̲e̲ ̲M̲o̲u̲n̲t̲ ̲K̲i̲l̲i̲m̲a̲n̲j̲a̲r̲o̲ ̲i̲n̲ ̲T̲a̲n̲z̲a̲n̲i̲a̲ ̲a̲n̲d̲ ̲o̲n̲ ̲t̲h̲e̲ ̲M̲o̲u̲n̲t̲ ̲K̲e̲n̲y̲a̲.̲
options: Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania and on Mount Kenya
the Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania and to Mount Kenya
Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania and the Mount Kenya
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania and on Mount Kenya "Mount Kilimanjaro" and "Mount Kenya" are peaks, the use of the definite article "the" before them is not necessary. Therefore, the correct option is indeed: Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania and on Mount Kenya So, the full corrected sentence would be: "Africa has glaciers on Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania and on Mount Kenya."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words. Recovering from an illness or medical treatment.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate one-word substitution for "Recovering from an illness or medical treatment" is: Convalescent Connoisseur: A person who is knowledgeable or discerning in a particular field, especially in matters of art, food, or wine. Cosmopolitan: Familiar with and at ease in many different countries and cultures. Bohemian: A person who lives an unconventional lifestyle, often associated with artistic or unconventional pursuits, free-spiritedness, and disregard for conventional norms or standards. Convalescent: A person who is recovering from an illness, injury, or medical treatment; the process of recovering health and strength after an illness or medical treatment.

questions.question: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. If a product placement P. we are either less likely to notice the placement Q. our attention is fixated on what will happen next, R. or more likely to be annoyed by it if we do notice it S. appears at the climax, the moment when
questions.explanation: The correct order of the parts of the sentence is: SQPR So, the rearranged sentence is: "If a product placement appears at the climax, the moment when our attention is fixated on what will happen next, we are either less likely to notice the placement or more likely to be annoyed by it if we do notice it."

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Through the (1) _______ , lonely years of my childhood, when my father’s palace (2) ______ to tighten its grip (3) _______ me until I couldn’t (4) _______, I would go to my nurse and ask for a story. And though she knew many wondrous and edifying tales, the one I made her tell me over and over was the (5) _______ of my birth. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 1.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 1 is: Long Menial: Relating to or suitable for a servant or low-ranking worker; involving tedious or unskilled labor. Long: Extending for a considerable distance from end to end Beautiful: Pleasing the senses or mind aesthetically. Timid: Showing a lack of courage or confidence; easily frightened; shy or reserved.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Through the (1) _______ , lonely years of my childhood, when my father’s palace (2) ______ to tighten its grip (3) _______ me until I couldn’t (4) _______, I would go to my nurse and ask for a story. And though she knew many wondrous and edifying tales, the one I made her tell me over and over was the (5) _______ of my birth. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 2.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 2 is: Seemed Seemed: Gave the impression of being something or having a particular quality; appeared or looked as if. Perhaps: Used to express uncertainty or possibility; maybe; it is possible that. Besides: In addition to; apart from; also; moreover.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Through the (1) _______ , lonely years of my childhood, when my father’s palace (2) ______ to tighten its grip (3) _______ me until I couldn’t (4) _______, I would go to my nurse and ask for a story. And though she knew many wondrous and edifying tales, the one I made her tell me over and over was the (5) _______ of my birth. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 3.
questions.explanation: The Correct Answer is: around Explanation: • In the passage, it mentions the palace trying to tighten its grip around the narrator, which correctly fits the context of something closing in or encircling. • Revolving means spinning, which does not fit well with the idea of tightening a grip. • Encompassing means including or covering, but it doesn’t convey the right sense of pressure or constriction here. • Surround is a verb and would not grammatically fit in this blank where a preposition like around is needed.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Through the (1) _______ , lonely years of my childhood, when my father’s palace (2) ______ to tighten its grip (3) _______ me until I couldn’t (4) _______, I would go to my nurse and ask for a story. And though she knew many wondrous and edifying tales, the one I made her tell me over and over was the (5) _______ of my birth. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 4.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 4 is: Breathe In this context, the sentence is describing a feeling of being suffocated or oppressed by the father's palace, suggesting that the speaker felt as though they couldn't breathe. The verb "breathe" in its base form is the correct choice here because it is used to convey the inability to perform the action of breathing due to the tightening grip of the palace.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Through the (1) _______ , lonely years of my childhood, when my father’s palace (2) ______ to tighten its grip (3) _______ me until I couldn’t (4) _______, I would go to my nurse and ask for a story. And though she knew many wondrous and edifying tales, the one I made her tell me over and over was the (5) _______ of my birth. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 5.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 5 is: Story The word "story" fits best in this context as it refers to a narrative or account of events, which the nurse would tell the speaker to provide comfort or distraction. The other options, such as "ode," "news," and "poem," do not convey the same idea of a narrative or account of events, making "story" the most appropriate choice.
